description: Extract geometric properties from shapes. Returns point coordinates or numeric values depending on the property. Use p(shape, propertyName) for simple properties, p(shape, propertyName, param) for indexed/parameterized properties, and p(shape1, shape2, propertyName) for two-shape operations. Chain p() calls to extract coordinates from point-valued properties: p(p(line, start), x).
utility: Extract properties (coordinates, lengths, angles, areas, foci, roots, etc.) from any shape

arguments.name: shape
arguments.type: point | line | vector | circle | polygon | rect | square | ellipse | parabola | hyperbola | angle | plot
arguments.description: Shape variable to extract property from

arguments.name: propertyName
arguments.description: Property to extract — e.g., x, y, distance, slope, area, foci, roots, sin, cos, tan

arguments.name: param
arguments.description: Index or parameter for indexed properties — e.g., p(polygon, corner, 1) for first corner, p(ellipse, foci, 2) for second focus

alternates.description: Get x or y coordinate of a point

alternates.syntax: p(line, distance) | p(line, slope) | p(line, angle)
alternates.description: Length, slope (rise/run), or angle from x-axis

alternates.syntax: p(p(line, start), x)  or  p(p(line, center), y)
alternates.description: Extract coordinates from start, end, center, at_ratio, y_intercept, x_intercept

alternates.syntax: p(circle, radius) | p(circle, area) | p(circle, circumference)
alternates.description: Radius, diameter, area, circumference, or center coordinates

alternates.syntax: p(polygon, area) | p(polygon, perimeter) | p(polygon, angle, 0)
alternates.description: Area, perimeter, num_sides, corner(index), edge(index), angle(index)

alternates.syntax: p(triangle, in_radius) | p(triangle, circum_radius) | p(p(triangle, centroid), x)
alternates.description: Inradius, circumradius, centroid, incenter, circumcenter, orthocenter
Magnitude (length), direction (angle in degrees), start, end, unit vector

alternates.syntax: p(V1, V2, dot) | p(V1, V2, cross) | p(V1, V2, scalar_proj)
alternates.description: Dot product, cross product, vector/scalar projection between two vectors

alternates.syntax: p(ellipse, foci, 1) | p(ellipse, eccentricity) | p(parabola, directrix, 1)
alternates.description: Foci, vertex, covertex, directrix, eccentricity, latus_rectum, semimajor, semiminor
Angle measure in degrees, vertex coordinates

alternates.syntax: p(angle, sin) | p(angle, cos) | p(angle, tan) | p(angle, csc) | p(angle, sec) | p(angle, cot)
alternates.description: Trigonometric ratios of the angle: sine, cosine, tangent, cosecant, secant, cotangent

alternates.syntax: p(p(plot, roots, 1), x) | p(p(plot, y_intercept), y)
alternates.description: Roots, y-intercept, critical_points, inflection_points, at_x (all return points — chain with x/y)
Angle between two lines or vectors in degrees

examples.expression:
x_val = p(P, x)
y_val = p(P, y)

d = p(L, distance)
m = p(L, slope)

x1 = p(p(L, start), x)
y1 = p(p(L, start), y)
x2 = p(p(L, end), x)
y2 = p(p(L, end), y)

mx = p(p(L, center), x)
my = p(p(L, center), y)

r = p(C, radius)
A = p(C, area)

h = p(p(C, center), x)
k = p(p(C, center), y)

a = p(p(T, edge, 0), distance)
b = p(p(T, edge, 1), distance)
c = p(p(T, edge, 2), distance)

A = p(T, angle, 0)
B = p(T, angle, 1)
C = p(T, angle, 2)

r = p(T, in_radius)
IC = p(T, incenter)
circle(G, IC, r)

mag = p(V, magnitude)
dir = p(V, direction)

F1 = point(G, p(E, foci, 1), c(red))
F2 = point(G, p(E, foci, 2), c(red))

root_x = p(p(pl, roots, 1), x)
y_int = p(p(pl, y_intercept), y)

examples.scenario: Use in desc() template for dynamic display
Use in computedproperty for named variable

examples.expression:
s = p(angle_1, sin)
c = p(angle_1, cos)
t = p(angle_1, tan)

s = p(angle_1, sin)
c = p(angle_1, cos)
t = p(angle_1, tan)
csc_val = p(angle_1, csc)
sec_val = p(angle_1, sec)
cot_val = p(angle_1, cot)

notes: Point-valued properties (start, end, center, foci, corner, roots, etc.) return points — chain with p() to get x or y: p(p(shape, prop), x)
Line-valued properties (edge, directrix) return lines — chain with p() to get distance, slope, etc.
Indexed properties use a numeric parameter: p(polygon, corner, 0) for first corner (0-based), p(ellipse, foci, 1) for first focus (1-based for conics)
Angle trig properties: sin, cos, tan, csc, sec, cot — compute the trigonometric ratio from the angle's measure in radians
Template syntax for desc(): use :{expression}:.2f to display computed values with formatting
